layman_headline: Market fear is thick — patience beats impulse right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful state with most altcoins bleeding. While a few coins show interesting chart patterns, none present a clean setup with strong conviction. Bitcoin is flat near $64K with high dominance at 56.4%, meaning money is flowing away from smaller coins into BTC. The best approach right now is to wait for a clearer entry point rather than force a trade into weakness.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines and keep cash ready. Watch Ethereum closely — if it dips to $1,800 support with signs of bouncing (like a hammer candle or RSI dropping below 35), that could be the entry worth taking.
expert_reasoning: Every candidate has significant flaws: BONK has a -2.0 chart bias with Three Black Crows — catching that knife is reckless. PUMP is up 30% in a week with no clear MA support structure. LDO rallied 52% in 5 days and has a terrible 0.38x risk/reward to resistance. 币安人生 is too illiquid and exotic. ETH at RSI 39 approaching $1,800 support is the most structurally sound setup, but the chart bias is still -0.7 with price below all major MAs. I need to see either RSI drop to ~35 or a confirmed bounce off $1,800 before committing capital. With BTC dominance at 56.4% and Fear at 29, alts remain under pressure — the improving fear trend (25.9 → 29) is encouraging but not yet decisive.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.4% continues to compress altcoin valuations — a further rise to 58%+ would drag ETH and all alts lower
Tether USDT regulatory countdown (2-year threat per CoinDesk) could create a systemic liquidity shock across all crypto
ETH chart structure is bearish (below all MAs) — $1,800 support failure would open a move toward $1,650
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a daily close below invalidates the bounce thesis
Fear & Greed index — a move above 35 would confirm improving sentiment and support entries
US CLARITY Act progress this week (per CoinTelegraph) — regulatory catalyst could move the entire market
market_context: Bitcoin consolidating near $64K with high dominance while the broader market sits in Fear territory at 29. Altcoins are under pressure but sentiment is slowly improving from last week's 25.9 average, suggesting we may be near a local bottom.
context_banner: Fear is improving but no clean swing setup exists yet — discipline means waiting for the right pitch.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is the closest to oversold among quality candidates — historically our 80% win rate signal, but needs to drop a few more points for conviction.
Fear & Greed at 29 with improving trend from 25.9 weekly average suggests we're near a sentiment bottom, but not yet at the 'extreme fear' levels that produce the best entries.
ETH derivatives reset coverage suggests overleveraged positions have been flushed, creating a cleaner base for the next move.
Potential US CLARITY Act vote this week could serve as a major catalyst for the entire crypto market — worth monitoring before committing capital.
Tether's USDT facing a 2-year regulatory countdown creates systemic risk that warrants caution on large new positions.
